problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Vagus nerve stimulation modulating brain-gut axis
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Vagus. Vagus nerve stimulation modulating brain-gut axis directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: fMRI connectivity study with 13 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The important thing is not to stop questioning. — Einstein
